title: Anil caloneura
intro: Anil caloneura, also known as Kalonji or Black Cumin, is a flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, native to the Middle East and South Asia. The seeds of Anil caloneura have been used in traditional medicine for their numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Anil caloneura seeds include thymoquinone, demethoxythymoquinone, and carvacrol, which are responsible for their medicinal properties. Anil caloneura seeds are commonly used in herbal preparations such as oils, capsules, and teas, as well as in traditional dishes and recipes in many Middle Eastern and Asian cultures.
